recentTweets.text:
Terrible news of yet another year of water shortages in #IRAQ. 

This story I spent months putting together last year explains why a water-sharing agreement with riparian neighbours is so important, key out of crisis. 

https://t.co/jfdNoWccsw

MUST READ: 1000s of Ukrainian civilians are being detained across Russia and the Ukrainian territories it occupies. Accounts confirm system of abuse, in violation of Geneva Conventions.

Important + meticulous work from @lhinnant @h_arhirova @VasilisaUKR 

https://t.co/YwMsRef30A

UPDATE: I will be joining @AP's Kyiv bureau as Ukraine correspondent. 

It's the most challenging assignment I've had so far, but I am lucky to be part of a dedicated and courageous team. A true privilege.

Our latest, the 1st since I assumed the new role:
https://t.co/SMQeYzdugm

If there’s one story you read today, let it be this stunning investigation by @RenataBritoAP + @felipedana tracing the tragic journey of a boat carrying migrants from an African city that ended up in the Caribbean.
“HELLO, IS IT ME YOU’RE LOOKING FOR?” https://t.co/kb9chjC7r7
Scene in transit. #Ukraine https://t.co/VFDHy4abpQ

Our story, about how an unlicensed ‘Starbucks’ in Baghdad is part of a growing IP problem in Iraq - the owner allegedly threatened lawyers after the coffee chain tried to take legal action. 

More: 

https://t.co/4SiZLoby44

Iran vs US match from a packed cafe in #Baghdad. A game, spectators joke, not being played on Iraqi soil, this time. 

#WorldCup https://t.co/6GnvKa13kb

Starkly different realities play out along the Tigris-Euphrates river basin.
 
Turkey + Iraq say they must cooperate to preserve the river system, among the most vulnerable.
 
But political failures and intransigence conspire to prevent a deal. (1/33)

https://t.co/8aWtWWr2p5
Good morning from #DhiQar: https://t.co/3alsU3ZN29

#IRAQ:

Grim day. At least 3 dead in clashes between @Mu_AlSadr followers and Iraqi forces, after he declared exit from politics. Tear gas, live ammunition used.

UN calls for peace: “The very survival of the state is at stake” 

Long night ahead...
https://t.co/H70uhdCoKU

A very thorough piece here by @lcmporter and @iraqoilreport team on the impact of dust storms on Iraq's most vulnerable, and the political inaction and failures that are making matters worse. 

https://t.co/UTXHX3IW61

#IRAQ: 

Good morning from the seat of Iraq’s parliament speaker, today: https://t.co/Xve6w7GLWH

#IRAQ:

Second time this week, 100s of followers of @Mu_AlSadr have scaled and brought down cement barriers leading the Green Zone. 

“We responded to al-Sadr's call. We will go to the Green Zone. No matter the cost.”

#BREAKING 

#IRAQ: British citizen Jim Fitton gets 15 years over smuggling artifacts via @AP

Verdict shocked those at court, including his defense attorney who expected 1 year as a “worst case scenario”
On the road in Muthanna. #IRAQ: https://t.co/9pj3f0wYJS

#IRAQ: 

Water shortages strain wheat harvest. 

“We need water to solve the problem of desertification, but we also need water to secure our food supplies... We don't have enough to do both,” - senior official at the Environment Ministry. 

https://t.co/ufGuYOINKa
Good morning #Baghdad. https://t.co/KZCAMcjs2q

BIG NEWS! 

@AP announces @zkaram, News Director for Lebanon, Syria and Iraq, named Deputy News Director for Europe. To focus on elevating AP's coverage of stories such as war in #Ukraine, future of Putin's Russia, cost of living crisis, migration, climate change.

#IRAQ: 

- Army says it now controls Sinjar, urges return of displaced

- In camps, internally displaced Yazidis reluctant to return citing ongoing hostilities between military and YBS. 

- “We don’t know where to go, we don’t have a place to go..."

https://t.co/PdgIQXi3pH

#IRAQ: 

- Clashes in Sinjar between Iraqi army and PKK-affiliated YBS (comprised mostly of local Yazidis) have lead thousands to flee

- At least 646 families (+3,000 ppl) have entered Dohuk province from Sinjar per local officials. 

https://t.co/L8qMqHvhRi

Bye for now #Afghanistan. Always bittersweet to leave these beautiful landscapes and mountain views. Stories out soon.

I’m coming back for you #Iraq. https://t.co/92hcdIlINX

Corpses lie in the streets of Mariupol. Hungry people break into stores in search of food and melt snow for water. Thousands huddle in basements, trembling at the sound of Russian shells pounding this strategic port city.

“Why shouldn’t I cry?” 

https://t.co/NUOfPSFSEB

I am in awe of my brave + tireless @AP colleagues reporting under very tough conditions in Ukraine these past weeks, in Russia, where it has become extremely difficult to report the news, and across Europe + ME + the world. No one untouched by these events, proud of their work.
Takes my breath away every time. Hello again, Kabul. On assignment for a few weeks. https://t.co/vFNrHAffi3
What a fantastic story by @janearraf:

A great read summarizing the complex dynamics at play in northern Iraq that could fuel an IS resurgence. @JohnDrtrs reports from Sinjar to Diyala where a similar pattern plays out: 

"But as long as political disputes aren't solved, Daesh will come back."

https://t.co/8NZDjZ70yg

Latest: Busy week in northern  #Iraq following up those repatriated from #Belarus. 

The Kalos, Yazidis displaced since 2014: 

“If it was my last day on this Earth, I will spend it trying to leave”

https://t.co/gqIdZAeP3u

Latest: 

In a #Kabul hospital, doctors and Taliban clash. 

When a 22-year old fighter was appointed to supervise, doctors were dismayed.

They demand salaries amid shortages. Their new boss wants to build a mosque. The rest will follow, he tells them.
https://t.co/3xa0VoNkIh

Latest: 

From a bird’s eye view, the village of Salar is camouflaged against a mountain range in Wardak. 

The community serves as a microcosm of the latest chapter in #Afghanistan’s history. 

Changes are afoot, beginning with a girl's school.

https://t.co/zsHTP1vUdz

Elegant writing and great reporting by @leloveluck + @Mustafa_salimb here. Beautiful photos by @emalfatto:

"Buffaloes fell sick. Fish were found dead on the shore"

https://t.co/4RZdM9Fka5
Well, this is different. https://t.co/KWfKLEfVmA

In #Iraq’s Electricity Ministry tacit partnerships between parties, bureaucrats and businessmen are forged to siphon state funds allocated to companies contracted to improve services. They line party coffers.

But this story is about more than electricity.
https://t.co/ybNutoivT3

With hundreds rushing the tarmac of Kabul's international airport desperate to flee the return of the Taliban, a young Afghan woman stood in limbo between two worlds. 

“I didn’t deserve this. No one deserves this,” she told me.

https://t.co/Ogwl6zeHQZ

“I am in big shock... How can it be possible for me as a woman who has worked so hard and tried to learn and advance, to now have to hide myself and stay at home?”

Afghan women fear return to 'dark days' amid Taliban sweep. By @zkaram and @ahseir. 

https://t.co/47E0edmG8P

#EXCLUSIVE 

Ahead of Washington trip, PM @MAKadhimi tells @AP Iraq no longer in need of U.S. combat forces. Deadline for withdrawal depends on talks. Iraq will still ask for U.S. training, more.

Many expect deadline to be set for the end of year. 

https://t.co/alJ8NZdM6D

Our latest: 

No beds, meds running low and hospitals prone to fire -  Iraqi doctors say they are losing the battle against coronavirus amid a third wave. 

And this was true well before the blaze that killed dozens in a COVID-19 isolation ward this week.

https://t.co/KTdJRpStQH

#EXCLUSIVE: In rare interview, commander of Kataib Sayyid al-Shuhada tells @AP they will retaliate against the US for the deaths of 4 of his men in an airstrike along the Iraq-Syria border last month. 

“We want an operation that befits those martyrs,”

https://t.co/6VuaO433WB

#IRAQ latest: 

- Widespread outages impact Iraq. MoE data shows total generation now at 4,700 MW. Generation on average around 20,000. 

- The cuts impacted even affluent areas of Baghdad where residents can have up to 24hrs of electricity. 

- No immediate reason given for cut.

A little late, but here’s our latest: 

We follow one environmentalist on a 12-hour mission crisscrossing #IRAQ’s iconic wetlands in search Maxwell’s smooth-coated otter, an  endangered and endemic species whose existence is vital to the wetlands. 

https://t.co/AVmAekMNxo

#IRAQ latest:

100 Iraqi families living in Syria's al-Hol camp were returned to Iraq, transferred in nearly a dozen buses yesterday night to Jadah camp in Qayara. 

Locals are concerned: “This decision will impact the community, people are very afraid” 

https://t.co/fvDLFxJ44k

Statement from @AP President and CEO Gary Pruitt on the Israeli attack against our bureau in Gaza: 

"The world will know less about what is happening in Gaza because of what happened today."

https://t.co/CqoDxogAJ3
This was also @AP's bureau in Gaza. Colleagues had to rush out after receiving the warning. It housed other media orgs, private homes.

We at the AP Baghdad Bureau are heartbroken over the passing of our dear colleague Khodeir Majid, who died of complications from COVID-19 this morning. 

May he Rest In Peace. 

https://t.co/KIkQd48POn

At the height of the protests, I interviewed activists abducted and released by suspected militia groups. What were they asked? "Who is your leader? Who is Joker?" 

In this documentary @SimonaFoltyn sets new standards for excellence, and answers this question. Superbly done.

I remember vividly, we were in the car on the way to meet his lawyer, and I asked him, aren't you afraid?

"I am afraid...But I lost what was most valuable to me, so I’ve got nothing else to lose.”

This was our story, I was honoured he let me tell it: https://t.co/FFt6tTGPX4

He would often take the six-hour long bus from his rural town in Missan to Baghdad to meet his lawyer.

Always, he had in hand the documents he hoped would bring to account an Iran-backed militia suspected of kidnapping his son. 

And always, he spoke of how much he missed Ali.

Jasb Aboud Hattab, father of missing activist Ali Jasb, who's quest for justice we documented, was shot dead, prelim investigation shows. 

Perpetrators not yet known. 

Unlike other families of the missing, Jasb was outspoken, always ready to tell you about his son.
Such an important observation. Question now is post #PopeinIRAQ, what happens to hope and expectations visit created?
And this is UR, lovely drone photos by AP's Nabil al-Jurani. https://t.co/G0XyOjxqdS

@Pontifex Processing the weight of these words in #IRAQ, given scale of past horror, calamity.  Was moved. 

@Pontifex: Peace does not demand winners or losers, but rather brothers and sisters who, for all the misunderstandings and hurts of the past, are journeying from conflict to unity.

.@Pontifex speaks from Ur, near the ziggurat, convenes inter-faith meeting.

“From this place, where faith was born, from the land of our father Abraham, let us affirm that God is merciful and that the greatest blasphemy is to profane his name by hating our brothers and sisters."

Details from mtg:
- Actual face-to-face 40 min, Sistani spoke for most
- Pope took shoes off, Sistani rose to greet
- Pope served tea +water, drank latter
- Some COVID concern, as Pope has met many people. Sistani not vaccinated.
- When he left, Pope took one last look of room
HISTORY. #PopeinIRAQ https://t.co/kh3b4i6C0i

IT BEGINS. @Pontifex due to arrive in #IRAQ in hours. 

Security forces deployed, Joint Ops spox says in line with plan. "This visit is really important to us". 

Here is a laydown of what is to come, and more updates to follow. 

https://t.co/u9AS93tlIh

Latest: Ahead of @Pontifex visit to Iraq, a survivor recalls the deadly militant attack on a Baghdad church in 2010. The carnage that day shook Iraq’s Christians to the core, fueled their exodus.

Why did he stay?

“Because this is my country,”

https://t.co/FORmFh08FC

Latest ahead of @Pontifex's visit:

Inside the meeting to take place between the Pope and Grand Ayatollah Ali al-Sistani. Details scrutinized, everything from shoes to seating.

The encounter will resonate across Iraq, even crossing borders into Iran.

https://t.co/jJ04Bx9dlI

This story, I've wanted to do for a long time. 

In #Iraq's south, a handful of women have become trailblazers in the country’s oil industry by taking up the grueling work at rig sites.  

Often they are asked what — as women — they are doing there.

https://t.co/XVzDe9XNfy

#SCOOP: 

Explosive-laden drones that targeted Saudi Arabia’s royal palace in the kingdom’s capital last month were launched from inside Iraq, senior Iran-backed militia official tells @AP 

(From the great @qabdulzahra, with @robertburnsAP) 

https://t.co/eU4mH98H0G

#SCOOP: 

Explosive-laden drones that targeted Saudi Arabia’s royal palace in the kingdom’s capital last month were launched from inside Iraq, senior Iran-backed militia official tells @AP 

(From the great @qabdulzahra, with @robertburnsAP) 

https://t.co/eU4mH98H0G

#IRAQ latest:

Iraq's top Catholic official confirms Pope Francis to meet Grand Ayatollah Ali Al-Sistani in Najaf on March 6. Hugely symbolic for Christian minority.
Delegation traveling with pontiff to be vaccinated before trip.  (w @nwinfield in Rome)

https://t.co/rL0TjGWkCa

#IRAQ latest:

- Grim day as rare twin suicide bombing hits busy commercial area of #Baghdad. Latest: 32 dead, 110 wounded. (Health Min). 

- Security official say it is work of Islamic State group.

- No group has taken responsibility for the attack. 

https://t.co/6iVlZvzAhg

#Iraq's F-16 fleet no longer flying missions as part of the  U.S.-led coalition reports @CathyOtten for @iraqoilreport. Program ends as a result of mismanagement + corruption. Great report, following her investigation earlier: https://t.co/JHOAj2Z7ND

https://t.co/0ozzmrTphZ

#SCOOP: Iranian Gen. Esmail Ghaani, head of Quds Force, comes to #Baghdad with a message for Iran-backed militias: 

Be calm and cease attacks for now against the American presence in #Iraq. 

(With the great @qabdulzahra)

#IRAQ #BREAKING: Rockets hit #Baghdad Green Zone signalling an end to informal truce announced by Iran-backed militia in October. 

Four rockets launched. Intercepted by C-RAM system. Of them, one lands 600m from U.S. embassy. Two Iraqi security forces wounded, officials tell @AP

.@AP interviews #IRAQ's Finance Minister on implementation of White Paper for reform, liquidity crisis, devaluation potential.  

 “...there is recognition that unless oil prices go up miraculously, this is something we have to cope with and manage.”

https://t.co/oVE6qgwoKD

.@AP #EXCLUSIVE: Interview w #Iraq PM @MAKadhimi ahead US trip, where he meets @realDonaldTrump: 

“In the end, we will still need cooperation and assistance at levels that today might not require direct and military support, and support on the ground,”

https://t.co/6qe5NvZDsn

There wasn't a single #Iraq watcher who hadn't crossed paths with @hushamalhashimi. I saw him just days ago. Shaking as I filed this. Terrible loss.

Family says he was assassinated by gunmen on motorbikes. Friends say he received threats from militias.

https://t.co/sTwPpzmIxE

Incidentally, I've been @AP's Iraq correspondent now for 6 months. Time has flown by, the entire world has changed but I couldn't be prouder or more thankful to serve the Iraq story and work with the inimitable @qabdulzahra, and best editor and  mentor @zkaram, every single day.

#BREAKING via @AP: #Iraqi security officials say a barrage of rockets has hit Camp Taji base housing US troops in second attack this week.

More details to come...

#Iraq last 24hrs:
- #Najaf: 8 protesters shot dead as followers of @Mu_AlSadr storm sit-in. 
- #Baghdad: Students march to Tahrir square from edu ministry
- @APMiddleEast latest: Senior Iraqi military sources say orders not to cooperate with coalition.
https://t.co/63wq1Q4lec

"Rosie the Riveter has an Iraqi flag on her cheek; Vincent Van Gogh’s “Starry Night” has the Turkish Restaurant building in place of a cypress tree."

Lovely story by @Alissanyt:

https://t.co/wsVNIbsEiF

#Iraq latest: 
- @Mu_AlSadr withdraws support from anti-government protesters, prompting followers to pack, leave. 
- Hours later, security forces swoop in re-open vital roads, bridges
- Tents torched in #Basra, #Baghdad 
- 1 protester dead, 44 wounded 
https://t.co/IGhFmZpETs

@AP @qabdulzahra One official told @AP, al-Muhandis arrived to the airport in a convoy to receive Soleimani whose plane had arrived from either Lebanon or Syria. The airstrike occurred as soon as he descended from the plane to be greeted by al-Muhandis and his companions, killing them all.

#Iraq latest: 
- Death toll from last night’s violence to 25, 130 wounded. Unidentified men fired live rounds from cars, right as an electricity cut. 
- Came hours after US slapped sanctions on head of powerful Iran-backed militia group. 
https://t.co/d1UIoznLDp

Last 24 hours in #Iraq:
- 3 killed, 10 wounded in Baghdad roadside bombing, 1st incident since start of protests. 
- 3 killed in clashes in Baghdad square, tear gas and live rounds used.
- Sistani reiterates reform calls https://t.co/R7cT3OQdVf

Pleased to announce that I've joined the fantastic @AP Baghdad team as Iraq correspondent. Grateful to keep serving the Iraq story, especially during these uncertain times. Stakes are high, but I'm lucky to have so many brilliant journalists as colleagues.
https://t.co/cp33RTRusL

In #Bardarash camp, work ongoing to erect at least 200 tents to accommodate incoming refugees from NE #Syria. At least 700 people expected I’m told, and are waiting to be cleared by Asayish at borders. https://t.co/XmQleNtXWt

Well, that was fun. Proud to represent @iraqoilreport and moderate the last panel of first day of #IEF2019, with distinguished speakers from Basra Gas Co, Dana Gas, the Oil Ministry and others. Fruitful discussion! https://t.co/4SaCNCX7cV

SCOOP: U.S. Secretary of State @SecPompeo approved a waiver enabling #Iraq to continue gas + electricity imports from #Iran w/o running afoul of U.S. sanctions, two senior Iraqi officials said. @berendvh + @benlando have more: https://t.co/s1l20et1bZ
Hello #Baghdad! It’s good to be back! https://t.co/NN4gwboH8E

Fascinating interview by @iraqoilreport's great @MohammedHkalari of Zardasht Shangali, commander of the YBS, on complex dynamics in #Sinjar. In March, YBS + Iraqi Army traded gunfire, resulting in casualties. "It was mostly the result of misunderstandings"
https://t.co/kzqq4yvTF2
Friday reading break at @thestationiq, #Baghdad’s first co-working space. https://t.co/RH0P50kiTl

#BREAKING: The #US grants #Iraq another 90-day sanctions waiver, enabling energy imports critical for meeting domestic demand. Previous waiver set to expire Tuesday. New extension effective immediately. Follow @iraqoilreport for more updates. https://t.co/FaFTacfn1I
One by one, cement blast walls continue to come down in #Baghdad’s Green Zone, per PM @AdilAbdAlMahdi order. https://t.co/Yd6nu5YtbR

#Iraq President @BarhamSalih responds to #Trump statement that US troops in Iraq to spy on Iran: “Don’t burden Iraq with issues that don’t represent a priority for my country.” Said US mil presence based on legal agreement to fight terror, anything beyond needs review. #RCDForum

#Iraq culture moment: Osama’s sonorous voice echoes throughout the imposing interiors of the Abbasid fortress of Ukhaider, erected in 775 AD, just 50 km south of #Karbala. The acoustics were designed such that not a whisper would go unheard. https://t.co/pO7kNlXfZO
Strawberry season comes to #Baghdad. https://t.co/62wexfn1wC
Kirkuk is about coexistence, says this Turkmen voter. #IraqiElections2018 https://t.co/8oGj6wCRs6
Very fashion forward here in this voting station in #Kirkuk #IraqElections2018 https://t.co/80Ay2bblJG
This couple voted because "it's important for Kurds right now in Kirkuk" #IraqiElections2018 https://t.co/JvzYtXfAv8

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 65 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Samya Kullab. The social-media posture reads as pragmatic economic / energy signal because the strongest matched frame is energy, trade, or economic language. Top matched terms: Iraq / Baghdad / Kirkuk, Syria / Rojava, Oil / Energy, Yazidi / Sinjar.

tweets.text:
Terrible news of yet another year of water shortages in #IRAQ. 

This story I spent months putting together last year explains why a water-sharing agreement with riparian neighbours is so important, key out of crisis. 

https://t.co/jfdNoWccsw

MUST READ: 1000s of Ukrainian civilians are being detained across Russia and the Ukrainian territories it occupies. Accounts confirm system of abuse, in violation of Geneva Conventions.

Important + meticulous work from @lhinnant @h_arhirova @VasilisaUKR 

https://t.co/YwMsRef30A

UPDATE: I will be joining @AP's Kyiv bureau as Ukraine correspondent. 

It's the most challenging assignment I've had so far, but I am lucky to be part of a dedicated and courageous team. A true privilege.

Our latest, the 1st since I assumed the new role:
https://t.co/SMQeYzdugm

If there’s one story you read today, let it be this stunning investigation by @RenataBritoAP + @felipedana tracing the tragic journey of a boat carrying migrants from an African city that ended up in the Caribbean.
“HELLO, IS IT ME YOU’RE LOOKING FOR?” https://t.co/kb9chjC7r7
Scene in transit. #Ukraine https://t.co/VFDHy4abpQ

Our story, about how an unlicensed ‘Starbucks’ in Baghdad is part of a growing IP problem in Iraq - the owner allegedly threatened lawyers after the coffee chain tried to take legal action. 

More: 

https://t.co/4SiZLoby44

Iran vs US match from a packed cafe in #Baghdad. A game, spectators joke, not being played on Iraqi soil, this time. 

#WorldCup https://t.co/6GnvKa13kb

Starkly different realities play out along the Tigris-Euphrates river basin.
 
Turkey + Iraq say they must cooperate to preserve the river system, among the most vulnerable.
 
But political failures and intransigence conspire to prevent a deal. (1/33)

https://t.co/8aWtWWr2p5
Good morning from #DhiQar: https://t.co/3alsU3ZN29

#IRAQ:

Grim day. At least 3 dead in clashes between @Mu_AlSadr followers and Iraqi forces, after he declared exit from politics. Tear gas, live ammunition used.

UN calls for peace: “The very survival of the state is at stake” 

Long night ahead...
https://t.co/H70uhdCoKU

A very thorough piece here by @lcmporter and @iraqoilreport team on the impact of dust storms on Iraq's most vulnerable, and the political inaction and failures that are making matters worse. 

https://t.co/UTXHX3IW61

#IRAQ: 

Good morning from the seat of Iraq’s parliament speaker, today: https://t.co/Xve6w7GLWH

#IRAQ:

Second time this week, 100s of followers of @Mu_AlSadr have scaled and brought down cement barriers leading the Green Zone. 

“We responded to al-Sadr's call. We will go to the Green Zone. No matter the cost.”

#BREAKING 

#IRAQ: British citizen Jim Fitton gets 15 years over smuggling artifacts via @AP

Verdict shocked those at court, including his defense attorney who expected 1 year as a “worst case scenario”
On the road in Muthanna. #IRAQ: https://t.co/9pj3f0wYJS

#IRAQ: 

Water shortages strain wheat harvest. 

“We need water to solve the problem of desertification, but we also need water to secure our food supplies... We don't have enough to do both,” - senior official at the Environment Ministry. 

https://t.co/ufGuYOINKa
Good morning #Baghdad. https://t.co/KZCAMcjs2q

BIG NEWS! 

@AP announces @zkaram, News Director for Lebanon, Syria and Iraq, named Deputy News Director for Europe. To focus on elevating AP's coverage of stories such as war in #Ukraine, future of Putin's Russia, cost of living crisis, migration, climate change.

#IRAQ: 

- Army says it now controls Sinjar, urges return of displaced

- In camps, internally displaced Yazidis reluctant to return citing ongoing hostilities between military and YBS. 

- “We don’t know where to go, we don’t have a place to go..."

https://t.co/PdgIQXi3pH

#IRAQ: 

- Clashes in Sinjar between Iraqi army and PKK-affiliated YBS (comprised mostly of local Yazidis) have lead thousands to flee

- At least 646 families (+3,000 ppl) have entered Dohuk province from Sinjar per local officials. 

https://t.co/L8qMqHvhRi

Bye for now #Afghanistan. Always bittersweet to leave these beautiful landscapes and mountain views. Stories out soon.

I’m coming back for you #Iraq. https://t.co/92hcdIlINX

Corpses lie in the streets of Mariupol. Hungry people break into stores in search of food and melt snow for water. Thousands huddle in basements, trembling at the sound of Russian shells pounding this strategic port city.

“Why shouldn’t I cry?” 

https://t.co/NUOfPSFSEB

I am in awe of my brave + tireless @AP colleagues reporting under very tough conditions in Ukraine these past weeks, in Russia, where it has become extremely difficult to report the news, and across Europe + ME + the world. No one untouched by these events, proud of their work.
Takes my breath away every time. Hello again, Kabul. On assignment for a few weeks. https://t.co/vFNrHAffi3
What a fantastic story by @janearraf:

A great read summarizing the complex dynamics at play in northern Iraq that could fuel an IS resurgence. @JohnDrtrs reports from Sinjar to Diyala where a similar pattern plays out: 

"But as long as political disputes aren't solved, Daesh will come back."

https://t.co/8NZDjZ70yg

Latest: Busy week in northern  #Iraq following up those repatriated from #Belarus. 

The Kalos, Yazidis displaced since 2014: 

“If it was my last day on this Earth, I will spend it trying to leave”

https://t.co/gqIdZAeP3u

Latest: 

In a #Kabul hospital, doctors and Taliban clash. 

When a 22-year old fighter was appointed to supervise, doctors were dismayed.

They demand salaries amid shortages. Their new boss wants to build a mosque. The rest will follow, he tells them.
https://t.co/3xa0VoNkIh

Latest: 

From a bird’s eye view, the village of Salar is camouflaged against a mountain range in Wardak. 

The community serves as a microcosm of the latest chapter in #Afghanistan’s history. 

Changes are afoot, beginning with a girl's school.

https://t.co/zsHTP1vUdz

Elegant writing and great reporting by @leloveluck + @Mustafa_salimb here. Beautiful photos by @emalfatto:

"Buffaloes fell sick. Fish were found dead on the shore"

https://t.co/4RZdM9Fka5
Well, this is different. https://t.co/KWfKLEfVmA

In #Iraq’s Electricity Ministry tacit partnerships between parties, bureaucrats and businessmen are forged to siphon state funds allocated to companies contracted to improve services. They line party coffers.

But this story is about more than electricity.
https://t.co/ybNutoivT3

With hundreds rushing the tarmac of Kabul's international airport desperate to flee the return of the Taliban, a young Afghan woman stood in limbo between two worlds. 

“I didn’t deserve this. No one deserves this,” she told me.

https://t.co/Ogwl6zeHQZ

“I am in big shock... How can it be possible for me as a woman who has worked so hard and tried to learn and advance, to now have to hide myself and stay at home?”

Afghan women fear return to 'dark days' amid Taliban sweep. By @zkaram and @ahseir. 

https://t.co/47E0edmG8P

#EXCLUSIVE 

Ahead of Washington trip, PM @MAKadhimi tells @AP Iraq no longer in need of U.S. combat forces. Deadline for withdrawal depends on talks. Iraq will still ask for U.S. training, more.

Many expect deadline to be set for the end of year. 

https://t.co/alJ8NZdM6D

Our latest: 

No beds, meds running low and hospitals prone to fire -  Iraqi doctors say they are losing the battle against coronavirus amid a third wave. 

And this was true well before the blaze that killed dozens in a COVID-19 isolation ward this week.

https://t.co/KTdJRpStQH

#EXCLUSIVE: In rare interview, commander of Kataib Sayyid al-Shuhada tells @AP they will retaliate against the US for the deaths of 4 of his men in an airstrike along the Iraq-Syria border last month. 

“We want an operation that befits those martyrs,”

https://t.co/6VuaO433WB

#IRAQ latest: 

- Widespread outages impact Iraq. MoE data shows total generation now at 4,700 MW. Generation on average around 20,000. 

- The cuts impacted even affluent areas of Baghdad where residents can have up to 24hrs of electricity. 

- No immediate reason given for cut.

A little late, but here’s our latest: 

We follow one environmentalist on a 12-hour mission crisscrossing #IRAQ’s iconic wetlands in search Maxwell’s smooth-coated otter, an  endangered and endemic species whose existence is vital to the wetlands. 

https://t.co/AVmAekMNxo

#IRAQ latest:

100 Iraqi families living in Syria's al-Hol camp were returned to Iraq, transferred in nearly a dozen buses yesterday night to Jadah camp in Qayara. 

Locals are concerned: “This decision will impact the community, people are very afraid” 

https://t.co/fvDLFxJ44k

Statement from @AP President and CEO Gary Pruitt on the Israeli attack against our bureau in Gaza: 

"The world will know less about what is happening in Gaza because of what happened today."

https://t.co/CqoDxogAJ3
This was also @AP's bureau in Gaza. Colleagues had to rush out after receiving the warning. It housed other media orgs, private homes.

We at the AP Baghdad Bureau are heartbroken over the passing of our dear colleague Khodeir Majid, who died of complications from COVID-19 this morning. 

May he Rest In Peace. 

https://t.co/KIkQd48POn

At the height of the protests, I interviewed activists abducted and released by suspected militia groups. What were they asked? "Who is your leader? Who is Joker?" 

In this documentary @SimonaFoltyn sets new standards for excellence, and answers this question. Superbly done.

I remember vividly, we were in the car on the way to meet his lawyer, and I asked him, aren't you afraid?

"I am afraid...But I lost what was most valuable to me, so I’ve got nothing else to lose.”

This was our story, I was honoured he let me tell it: https://t.co/FFt6tTGPX4

He would often take the six-hour long bus from his rural town in Missan to Baghdad to meet his lawyer.

Always, he had in hand the documents he hoped would bring to account an Iran-backed militia suspected of kidnapping his son. 

And always, he spoke of how much he missed Ali.

Jasb Aboud Hattab, father of missing activist Ali Jasb, who's quest for justice we documented, was shot dead, prelim investigation shows. 

Perpetrators not yet known. 

Unlike other families of the missing, Jasb was outspoken, always ready to tell you about his son.
Such an important observation. Question now is post #PopeinIRAQ, what happens to hope and expectations visit created?
And this is UR, lovely drone photos by AP's Nabil al-Jurani. https://t.co/G0XyOjxqdS

@Pontifex Processing the weight of these words in #IRAQ, given scale of past horror, calamity.  Was moved. 

@Pontifex: Peace does not demand winners or losers, but rather brothers and sisters who, for all the misunderstandings and hurts of the past, are journeying from conflict to unity.

.@Pontifex speaks from Ur, near the ziggurat, convenes inter-faith meeting.

“From this place, where faith was born, from the land of our father Abraham, let us affirm that God is merciful and that the greatest blasphemy is to profane his name by hating our brothers and sisters."

Details from mtg:
- Actual face-to-face 40 min, Sistani spoke for most
- Pope took shoes off, Sistani rose to greet
- Pope served tea +water, drank latter
- Some COVID concern, as Pope has met many people. Sistani not vaccinated.
- When he left, Pope took one last look of room
HISTORY. #PopeinIRAQ https://t.co/kh3b4i6C0i

IT BEGINS. @Pontifex due to arrive in #IRAQ in hours. 

Security forces deployed, Joint Ops spox says in line with plan. "This visit is really important to us". 

Here is a laydown of what is to come, and more updates to follow. 

https://t.co/u9AS93tlIh

Latest: Ahead of @Pontifex visit to Iraq, a survivor recalls the deadly militant attack on a Baghdad church in 2010. The carnage that day shook Iraq’s Christians to the core, fueled their exodus.

Why did he stay?

“Because this is my country,”

https://t.co/FORmFh08FC

Latest ahead of @Pontifex's visit:

Inside the meeting to take place between the Pope and Grand Ayatollah Ali al-Sistani. Details scrutinized, everything from shoes to seating.

The encounter will resonate across Iraq, even crossing borders into Iran.

https://t.co/jJ04Bx9dlI

This story, I've wanted to do for a long time. 

In #Iraq's south, a handful of women have become trailblazers in the country’s oil industry by taking up the grueling work at rig sites.  

Often they are asked what — as women — they are doing there.

https://t.co/XVzDe9XNfy

#SCOOP: 

Explosive-laden drones that targeted Saudi Arabia’s royal palace in the kingdom’s capital last month were launched from inside Iraq, senior Iran-backed militia official tells @AP 

(From the great @qabdulzahra, with @robertburnsAP) 

https://t.co/eU4mH98H0G

#IRAQ latest:

Iraq's top Catholic official confirms Pope Francis to meet Grand Ayatollah Ali Al-Sistani in Najaf on March 6. Hugely symbolic for Christian minority.
Delegation traveling with pontiff to be vaccinated before trip.  (w @nwinfield in Rome)

https://t.co/rL0TjGWkCa

#IRAQ latest:

- Grim day as rare twin suicide bombing hits busy commercial area of #Baghdad. Latest: 32 dead, 110 wounded. (Health Min). 

- Security official say it is work of Islamic State group.

- No group has taken responsibility for the attack. 

https://t.co/6iVlZvzAhg

#Iraq's F-16 fleet no longer flying missions as part of the  U.S.-led coalition reports @CathyOtten for @iraqoilreport. Program ends as a result of mismanagement + corruption. Great report, following her investigation earlier: https://t.co/JHOAj2Z7ND

https://t.co/0ozzmrTphZ

#SCOOP: Iranian Gen. Esmail Ghaani, head of Quds Force, comes to #Baghdad with a message for Iran-backed militias: 

Be calm and cease attacks for now against the American presence in #Iraq. 

(With the great @qabdulzahra)

#IRAQ #BREAKING: Rockets hit #Baghdad Green Zone signalling an end to informal truce announced by Iran-backed militia in October. 

Four rockets launched. Intercepted by C-RAM system. Of them, one lands 600m from U.S. embassy. Two Iraqi security forces wounded, officials tell @AP

.@AP interviews #IRAQ's Finance Minister on implementation of White Paper for reform, liquidity crisis, devaluation potential.  

 “...there is recognition that unless oil prices go up miraculously, this is something we have to cope with and manage.”

https://t.co/oVE6qgwoKD

Latest: One evening last year, the lawyer Ali Jasb vanished into the night. He was forced into an SUV by two men.

Since then, his father's quest for justice has run against one major obstacle: The limits of the state in the face of powerful militias. 

https://t.co/FFt6tTGPX4

.@AP #EXCLUSIVE: Interview w #Iraq PM @MAKadhimi ahead US trip, where he meets @realDonaldTrump: 

“In the end, we will still need cooperation and assistance at levels that today might not require direct and military support, and support on the ground,”

https://t.co/6qe5NvZDsn

“At first we were asking them. We had demands. But today we’re not asking any more, we are acting, we are taking back what is ours.” by @LovedayM @LizSly and @SarahDadouch capturing the rage in #Beirut as thousands take to street in protest. 

https://t.co/4z6g4ewpOq
The Galata Tower this evening in solidarity with Lebanon. #BeirutBlast https://t.co/eUtyzV8Elm

There wasn't a single #Iraq watcher who hadn't crossed paths with @hushamalhashimi. I saw him just days ago. Shaking as I filed this. Terrible loss.

Family says he was assassinated by gunmen on motorbikes. Friends say he received threats from militias.

https://t.co/sTwPpzmIxE

"But the @AP determined... that the highest levels of leadership were directly involved in organizing an enslavement machine that became central to the group’s structure and identity." 

Outstanding by @seldeeb. 
https://t.co/vX7DY4Lngf

Incidentally, I've been @AP's Iraq correspondent now for 6 months. Time has flown by, the entire world has changed but I couldn't be prouder or more thankful to serve the Iraq story and work with the inimitable @qabdulzahra, and best editor and  mentor @zkaram, every single day.

#BREAKING via @AP: #Iraqi security officials say a barrage of rockets has hit Camp Taji base housing US troops in second attack this week.

More details to come...

#Iraq last 24hrs:
- #Najaf: 8 protesters shot dead as followers of @Mu_AlSadr storm sit-in. 
- #Baghdad: Students march to Tahrir square from edu ministry
- @APMiddleEast latest: Senior Iraqi military sources say orders not to cooperate with coalition.

"Rosie the Riveter has an Iraqi flag on her cheek; Vincent Van Gogh’s “Starry Night” has the Turkish Restaurant building in place of a cypress tree."

Lovely story by @Alissanyt:

https://t.co/wsVNIbsEiF

#Iraq latest: 
- @Mu_AlSadr withdraws support from anti-government protesters, prompting followers to pack, leave. 
- Hours later, security forces swoop in re-open vital roads, bridges
- Tents torched in #Basra, #Baghdad 
- 1 protester dead, 44 wounded 
https://t.co/IGhFmZpETs

@AP @qabdulzahra One official told @AP, al-Muhandis arrived to the airport in a convoy to receive Soleimani whose plane had arrived from either Lebanon or Syria. The airstrike occurred as soon as he descended from the plane to be greeted by al-Muhandis and his companions, killing them all.

Latest: “Everyone is terrified,” said a protester in Tahrir. “We don’t want this to become a street war. That is why we are trying to stay peaceful. But day after day we find that we are alone.” @AP #IraqProtests 
https://t.co/d1UIoznLDp

#Iraq latest: 
- Death toll from last night’s violence to 25, 130 wounded. Unidentified men fired live rounds from cars, right as an electricity cut. 
- Came hours after US slapped sanctions on head of powerful Iran-backed militia group. 
https://t.co/d1UIoznLDp

Worked at the @DailyStarLeb for almost three years - no wages last six months of 2015. Never occurred to us then to protest, we just kept showing up everyday. Amazed by TDS journalists on strike today. They have worked tirelessly to produce important work. Solidarity.

“General Strike!” Headline of #IraqProtests official newspaper “Tuk Tuk” published by a group of writers and printed nearby Tahrir Square. Named after powerful symbol of the protest movement, the tuk tuk drivers who ferry injured protesters from frontline to medical centers. https://t.co/qZj1Yr2ID2

Last 24 hours in #Iraq:
- 3 killed, 10 wounded in Baghdad roadside bombing, 1st incident since start of protests. 
- 3 killed in clashes in Baghdad square, tear gas and live rounds used.
- Sistani reiterates reform calls https://t.co/R7cT3OQdVf

Pleased to announce that I've joined the fantastic @AP Baghdad team as Iraq correspondent. Grateful to keep serving the Iraq story, especially during these uncertain times. Stakes are high, but I'm lucky to have so many brilliant journalists as colleagues.
https://t.co/cp33RTRusL

In #Bardarash camp, work ongoing to erect at least 200 tents to accommodate incoming refugees from NE #Syria. At least 700 people expected I’m told, and are waiting to be cleared by Asayish at borders. https://t.co/XmQleNtXWt

Well, that was fun. Proud to represent @iraqoilreport and moderate the last panel of first day of #IEF2019, with distinguished speakers from Basra Gas Co, Dana Gas, the Oil Ministry and others. Fruitful discussion! https://t.co/4SaCNCX7cV

SCOOP: U.S. Secretary of State @SecPompeo approved a waiver enabling #Iraq to continue gas + electricity imports from #Iran w/o running afoul of U.S. sanctions, two senior Iraqi officials said. @berendvh + @benlando have more: https://t.co/s1l20et1bZ
Hello #Baghdad! It’s good to be back! https://t.co/NN4gwboH8E

Fascinating interview by @iraqoilreport's great @MohammedHkalari of Zardasht Shangali, commander of the YBS, on complex dynamics in #Sinjar. In March, YBS + Iraqi Army traded gunfire, resulting in casualties. "It was mostly the result of misunderstandings"
https://t.co/kzqq4yvTF2
Friday reading break at @thestationiq, #Baghdad’s first co-working space. https://t.co/RH0P50kiTl

#BREAKING: The #US grants #Iraq another 90-day sanctions waiver, enabling energy imports critical for meeting domestic demand. Previous waiver set to expire Tuesday. New extension effective immediately. Follow @iraqoilreport for more updates. https://t.co/FaFTacfn1I
One by one, cement blast walls continue to come down in #Baghdad’s Green Zone, per PM @AdilAbdAlMahdi order. https://t.co/Yd6nu5YtbR

#Iraq President @BarhamSalih responds to #Trump statement that US troops in Iraq to spy on Iran: “Don’t burden Iraq with issues that don’t represent a priority for my country.” Said US mil presence based on legal agreement to fight terror, anything beyond needs review. #RCDForum

#Iraq culture moment: Osama’s sonorous voice echoes throughout the imposing interiors of the Abbasid fortress of Ukhaider, erected in 775 AD, just 50 km south of #Karbala. The acoustics were designed such that not a whisper would go unheard. https://t.co/pO7kNlXfZO
Strawberry season comes to #Baghdad. https://t.co/62wexfn1wC
